title: Reconcile the cancellation-cutoff docs
description: A customer complained about being charged a cancellation fee for a booking they cancelled 30 hours in advance - which README.md says should be free. Investigation shows the documentation and the code disagree about the free-cancellation cutoff: the service enforces a 48-hour cutoff (defined in internal/booking), while README.md and docs/architecture.md describe a different number. The code is the source of truth. Correct the documentation so every statement about the cutoff matches the enforced behavior, without changing any code.
acceptance_criteria: The cancellation policy section of README.md states the 48-hour cutoff the code enforces.
No statement contradicting the enforced cutoff remains anywhere in the repository's documentation.
No Go source file is changed.
Documentation content unrelated to the cutoff is left untouched.
go test ./... passes (trivially, since no code changed).
constraints: Documentation-only change; the constant in internal/booking is authoritative and must not be edited.
Preserve the existing style and structure of the documents; correct the facts, do not rewrite the prose.
non_goals: Changing the enforced cutoff or the fee tiers in code.
Documenting the fee tier percentages beyond what the docs already say.

visible_validation.description: Suite still passes; no code was touched.
Diff touches only Markdown documentation files.

expected_artifacts: A documentation-only diff against the starting revision

human_review_rubric.criterion: Every stale cutoff statement found and corrected; none invented
Zero code changes and zero unrelated doc changes
The doc-versus-code conflict was verified against the code before editing

known_ambiguities: Sentences that describe the tier structure without naming the cutoff may or may not need rewording; only statements that contradict the enforced behavior must change.
Whether to note the correction in CONTRIBUTING.md-style changelogs is open; the repository has no changelog, so no entry is expected.
expected_failure_modes: Changing the code to match the docs instead of the docs to match the code.
Missing the second document that repeats the stale number.
